title: Scene-Level Beat Sheet: Star Wars: Episode IV — A New Hope
work: Star Wars: Episode IV — A New Hope
creator: George Lucas
description: Maps every node of the Hero's Journey archetype to specific scenes in Star Wars: Episode IV, with timestamps and duration percentages. The film is one of the purest Hero's Journey adaptations in cinema.

scenes.description: Opening crawl and Rebel blockade runner captured by Star Destroyer
scenes.notes: Establishes the galactic conflict as background context before we meet Luke.

scenes.description: Luke Skywalker's life on the moisture farm — purchasing droids, dreaming of the academy, dinner with Uncle Owen and Aunt Beru
scenes.notes: The true Ordinary World: Luke is restless, trapped in routine, gazing at the twin sunset. Visual shorthand for longing.

scenes.description: R2-D2 plays Leia's holographic message — 'Help me, Obi-Wan Kenobi'
scenes.notes: The literal call. Luke sees a beautiful princess asking for help — the call combines duty, mystery, and desire.

scenes.description: R2-D2 escapes to find Obi-Wan; Luke pursues and encounters the Tusken Raiders
scenes.notes: The call draws Luke physically out of his ordinary world. The Tusken Raider attack is the first taste of danger.

scenes.description: Luke tells Obi-Wan he can't go to Alderaan — 'I've got work to do. It's not that I like the Empire, I hate it, but...'
scenes.notes: Classic refusal grounded in duty to family. Luke wants to go but feels obligated to stay. The refusal is brief — Lucas keeps momentum.

scenes.description: Obi-Wan's hut — reveals Luke's father was a Jedi, gives him the lightsaber, explains the Force
scenes.notes: Obi-Wan reframes Luke's identity: you are not a farmer's nephew, you are a Jedi's son. The lightsaber is the physical token of inheritance.

scenes.description: Discovery of the destroyed Jawa sandcrawler; Owen and Beru murdered by stormtroopers
scenes.notes: The refusal is overridden by destruction of the ordinary world. Luke's last ties to home are literally burned away. He commits to following Obi-Wan.

scenes.description: Mos Eisley cantina — 'You will never find a more wretched hive of scum and villainy'
scenes.notes: The cantina is the threshold between the familiar (farm) and the special world (galactic adventure). Alien creatures, violence, Han Solo — everything is new.

scenes.description: Han Solo negotiation and Millennium Falcon departure; escape from Mos Eisley under Imperial fire
scenes.notes: Physically crossing the threshold: leaving Tatooine. The jump to hyperspace is the definitive boundary crossing.

scenes.description: Lightsaber training with the blast shield helmet aboard the Falcon
scenes.notes: First trial: learning to trust the Force. 'Your eyes can deceive you — don't trust them.'

scenes.description: Arrival at Alderaan's debris field; captured by the Death Star's tractor beam
scenes.notes: Second trial: discovery that Alderaan is destroyed, and the Falcon is caught. The stakes escalate from rescue mission to survival.

scenes.description: Hiding in smuggling compartments; disguising as stormtroopers; splitting up inside the Death Star
scenes.notes: Infiltration trial: navigating enemy territory using cunning rather than force.

scenes.description: Luke discovers Leia is aboard and insists on rescuing her; Han reluctantly agrees
scenes.notes: The decision to go deeper into danger rather than escape. Luke overrides Han's pragmatism with idealism.

scenes.description: Detention block shootout and rescue of Leia; escape into the garbage compactor
scenes.notes: The descent into the literal belly of the beast. The garbage compactor is the lowest physical point — trapped, crushed, surrounded by filth.

scenes.description: Obi-Wan's duel with Darth Vader; Obi-Wan's death as Luke watches helplessly
scenes.notes: The supreme ordeal: Luke loses his mentor. Obi-Wan's sacrifice ('If you strike me down...') transforms the loss into a spiritual inheritance. Luke must now carry the Force alone.

scenes.description: Escape from the Death Star under TIE fighter attack; Luke mans the gun turret
scenes.notes: Immediate aftermath of the ordeal: Luke channels grief into action. His first real combat.

scenes.description: Arrival at the Rebel base on Yavin 4; delivery of the Death Star plans; battle briefing
scenes.notes: The reward is threefold: the Death Star plans (tactical), the Rebel Alliance community (belonging), and Luke's new identity as a fighter pilot (purpose). He has transformed from a farm boy to a soldier.

scenes.description: Trench run begins — Rebel fighters attack the Death Star; Gold Leader's failed run
scenes.notes: The road back is literal: flying toward certain death to destroy the threat. Other pilots fail and die, raising the stakes until only Luke remains.

scenes.description: Luke's trench run — Vader locks on, R2-D2 hit, 'Use the Force, Luke' — Han Solo returns to clear Vader; Luke fires the torpedoes
scenes.notes: The final death-and-rebirth moment. Luke turns off his targeting computer (trusting the Force over technology), is saved by Han's return (friendship over self-interest), and destroys the Death Star. He dies as a farm boy and is resurrected as a hero.

scenes.description: Death Star explodes; triumphant return to Yavin 4
scenes.notes: The explosion is the visible proof of victory — the elixir is safety for the Rebellion.

scenes.description: Medal ceremony — Leia awards medals to Luke and Han in the great hall
scenes.notes: The elixir made visible: recognition, community, purpose. Luke stands where he once dreamed of standing — at the center of something that matters. The throne room shot mirrors the twin sunset shot, but longing has been replaced by fulfillment.

_stats.unmapped_note: Remaining ~5% accounts for transitional shots, establishing sequences, and the Droids' subplot aboard the blockade runner (C-3PO and R2-D2 before Luke appears).
